mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, prompting market pricing of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals labor market cooling, which reduces wage-push inflation pressures and allows equity valuations to stabilize, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of threshold-crossing instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening household balance sheets and reduced construction demand, which contracts aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistically significant logit coefficient (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 2017 baseline index of 100, it confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in less than 45% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 100 during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 1.5% establishes a higher risk-free discount rate that compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a positive mean forward return over 60 days in greater than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment (PAYEMS) combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms genuine labor market strength without wage inflation overshoot, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 20-day returns across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP (GDPC1) reflects strong macroeconomic output that directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) stagnates while CPIAUCSL exceeds 235, supply constraints dominate demand, eroding profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to generate negative returns in greater than 55% of observations when both conditions are simultaneously met.
